chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Performed on Behalf of the Deceased Regarding the Pilgrimage of Islam, Whether They Made a Will or Not

text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ وَالِدَتِي تُوُفِّيَتْ وَ لَمْ تَحُجَّ قَالَ «يَحُجُّ عَنْهَا رَجُلٌ أَوِ اِمْرَأَةٌ» قَالَ قُلْتُ أَيُّهُمْ أَحَبُّ إِلَيْكَ قَالَ «رَجُلٌ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2921 - It was narrated from Basheer al-Nabbal, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "My mother passed away and did not perform Hajj."
Imam (as) said: "A man or a woman may perform Hajj on her behalf."
I asked: "Which is more preferable to you?"
Imam (as) said: "A man is more preferable to me."
